selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 7.4% and 20-day momentum is 17.8%.
Trend quality is supported by 80% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 9.1% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.8%, downside volatility near 1.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 5.2%.
Volume confirmation is 6.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.9% versus SPY over 5 days and 2.1% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.87, confidence 0.82, and 0.6 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.87, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.6 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.75, average source quality 0.77, confidence 0.82.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Price action and news are pointing in the same direction, which raises trust in the setup.
Overall model score is 0.282 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Is It Worth Investing in Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Directly about JNJ and analyst sentiment (ABR); may influence retail/inflow sentiment modestly but does not present new material developments (earnings, guidance, regulatory action) likely to change near-term fundamentals.

news_evidence.title: Vanguard Health Care vs. iShares Biotech: How Do These ETFs Stack Up?
news_evidence.llm_reason: ETF comparison is sector-level context; could marginally affect JNJ via sector flows, but contains no JNJ-specific news or catalysts relevant to the 1–10 day horizon.
